_doc: Editor steering notes for the conference preview agent. Authoritative — these override prior knowledge and any conflicting context-data interpretation. Keep each note to 1–3 short sentences. Empty strings are dropped silently. Loaded by build_conference_context.py from /cfb-research/writer_notes/<season>/<conf>.json.
_usage: conference_note: 1–3 sentences with two purposes — (a) the meta-narrative for the year (e.g., 'The realignment dust has settled but the talent gap between the top three and the rest is still widening.'), and (b) calibration the agent needs to right-size confidence: what 'good' looks like in this league. SEC example: 'In modern SEC: 7-1 in conf typically reaches the title game; 9-0 has never happened. 9 overall wins is a historic season.' MAC example: 'In the MAC, 8 conference wins is championship caliber; 6-2 contends.' Combine narrative + calibration when both matter.

team_notes.<slug>: 1–3 sentences about that specific team — what the data won't tell the agent (a coaching change, a critical transfer, an injury, an off-field story, the angle the writeup should take).
Slugs match the canonical team slugs in build_team_context.py / scrape_team_context.py.
conference_note: The AAC or American Athletic conference has rebranded as just 'American' which is both confusing and unnecessary. The AAC has been the top rated G6 conference, with Tulane making the CFP last season in 2025. Three storylines define the 2026 American: (1) Three of the AAC's strongest programs all lost their head coaches to P4 schools; (2) The AAC looks tightly compressed in 2026 with 8 or 9 teams capable of winning the conference championship (3) The bottom of the AAC is both well defined and well below the other schools. Tulane won the AAC in 2025, beating North Texas 34-21 in their home Yulman Stadium.  When team_notes give a 3-tier range (e.g., Tier 2-4), that's not a hedge — it's the league norm. The agent should NOT over-anchor on a single 'centering' tier; instead treat the range itself as the projection. A team at Tier 2-4 means 'either it works and they're contending for the title game or it doesn't and they're fighting for a bowl' — both branches are real. Conference USA tier framework (tiers describe team caliber, NOT exact record): Tier 1 = AAC title-game favorite, lower tier CFB bowl game contenders ; Tier 2 = title-game contender, lower tier bowl game contenders; Tier 3 = middle tier and title-game and bowl game outsider; Tier 4 = bottom of the conference where reaching bowl eligibility would itself be a tremendous achievement. When team_notes give a range, the agent should treat the wider boundary as the realistic downside — but per the volatility note above, 3-tier ranges are common here and the range itself should be framed as the projection rather than collapsing to a single tier. The American Championship game is played in the home stadium of the team with the best overall conference win percentage.

team_notes.army: Tier 2-3 AAC team; Army does not accept transfers due to military requirements, but has still fielded a consistent winning football team.  2024 AAC conference champions. HC Jeff Monken a really good one, OC & DC both entering 3rd year on staff. Run an option offense.  Senior QB Cale Hellums returns for his second year as starting QB, lead team in rushing last season.  RBs have some experience but replacing leaders from last season. Projected starting OL will be all seniors, four of which are returning starters from 2025; lead by OC Brady Small a top 10 rated OL by PFF.  Defense is replacing nearly everyone, biggest question on the team and will determine how far Army will go this fall.
team_notes.charlotte: Tier 4 AAC team; Charlotte has very little funding or noticable interest from its own administration to be good at football and it shows; have not won 6 games or more since 2019.  HC Tim Albin in his second season, he was a really good HC at Ohio University and it's still strange he took this job. Bottom 20 in returning production; QB Grayson Loftis was the starter last season but may lose his job to Conner Harrell who was the original 2025 starting QB until he was injured in week 4 and lost for the season - treat as unresolved. RBs are all portals that barely played last year at previous schools; although WR added some experienced and productive portal players like Jaden Barnes from App State.  The OL is reportedly heavier, whether it's better is a different story. The defense was awful last season, returns almost no one and added very little from the transfer portal.  Likely a bottom 10 defense again this fall. Projected last in AAC again this season.
team_notes.east-carolina: Tier 1-2 AAC team; 3rd year HC Blake Harrell has been good, won 8 games in 2024, 9 in 2025.  East Carolina is one of the favorites in the AAC this fall.  QB is unresolved, portal adds Mitch Griffis from Texas Tech is battling Emory Williams from Miami for QB1. WRs have been called a strength by coaches, Brock Spalding returns and Landon Sides from North Texas plus Ja'Keith Hamilton from FCS Furman are a good group.  RB has three with decent experience; OL replacing four starters but added three portal players that started last fall at previous schools.  Defense replacing a lot, but DL Preston Carr and Jasiyah Robinson are back and good, as is LB DJ Johnson Jr.  Secondary replacing a lot, genuine question.
team_notes.florida-atlantic: Tier 2-3 AAC team; Offense runs the air raid under HC Zach Kittley who also calls plays as the OC. QB Caden Veltkamp is back; one of the better QBs in the G6 but 22 turnovers last year was a real problem; and he missed a lot of spring ball while recovering from a shoulder injury.  FAU has a great stable of WR lead by Easton Messer and Dominique Henry; RB Kaden Shields-Dutton is back after sharing starting duties last fall.  OL looks really strong with 5 experienced starters, FAU has one of the most experienced OL's in the G6.  Offense should be fun this fall; Defense has been the problem.  However, FAU added some really experienced division II players like DEs Kenyon Garner + Jay Crable, plus LB Nate Fischer; all really great finds for DC Brett Dewhurst. The secondary has experience but wasn't very good.  How well the portal adds on defense work out will determine how far FAU goes, because the offense could carry them a long way; could be a surprise contender in the AAC.
team_notes.memphis: Tier 1 AAC team; Lost HC Ryan Silverfield to Arkansas; New HC Charles Huff hired from Southern Miss, brought 15 players from Southern Miss via the transfer portal.  QB portal additions Marcus Stokes from FCS West Florida is battling Air Noland from South Carolina for QB1 - treat as unsettled.  RB Jaylin Carter played just over 150 snaps last year at Southern Miss, other portal adds will fight for RB1.  WR is a mix of portals, TE Hunter Tipton signed from Middle Tennessee is a big, productive target.  OL has good potential with 7 players with starting experience, all portal additions, three from FCS. DE J'Mond Tapp from Southern Miss is a legit pass rush threat; Secondary a real strength with proven starters like CBs like Anthony Richard Jr. from Southern Miss and Darius Malcolm Jr. from FCS Wofford and safety Ian Foster from Southern Miss. Still an AAC favorite even with all the transition.
team_notes.navy: Tier 2-3 AAC team; Navy does not accept transfers due to military requirements, but has still fielded a consistent winning football team. HC Brian Newberry has been tremendous; has gone 21-5 the past two seasons; went 6-0 at home in 2025. OC Drew Cronic has been a great one; going into his 3rd year at Navy. QB Braxton Woodson is the starter for the first time; has backed up Blake Horvath who graduated after 2025 and a tremendous career.  Woodson has some experience and has been a dynamic runner when he's gotten on the field.  RB & WR are complete rebuilds, although they have two WR with some experience. OL looks strong with four returning starters; the offense will come down to Woodson settling in at QB and new RBs stepping up.  Defense replacing a lot as well; DL is on the lighter side and all new but DT Griffen Willis is a really good one. LBs return 3 productive starters, DBs return some decent parts.  Navy's defense is usually undersized, coaching has helped but only goes so far.  Still, Navy under Newberry has proven to keep finding replacements and winning games and I expect the same this fall until proven otherwise.
team_notes.north-texas: Tier 2-4 AAC team; Lost HC Eric Morris and a ton of players to the portal who went with Morris to Oklahoma State.  Hired HC Neal Brown, who was fired from West Virginia in 2024 but was a good HC at Troy, but that was 8 years ago.  QB will be Tayven Jackson who's on his 4th CFB program. RB Jahiem White was good at West Viriginia but missed most of 2025 with a knee injury; WR is mostly portal adds and the most experience comes from FCS adds like James Tyre.  The OL has one player with starting experience and he was added from the portal.  Defense was really bad last season and replaces most everyone.  The defense has experienced players with limited success from previous teams, a real unknown at this point.  UNT won 11 games last season, if they win half of that it would be a success with this roster.
team_notes.rice: Tier 4 AAC team; Rice is a high academic institution with real limitations on portal additions needing to meet their academic standards. HC Scott Abell in his second season, went to bowl at 5-7 last season; Abell runs a unique gun option offense he brought from FCS Davidson. Qb will be Jacurri Brown, a portall add from UCF who's been a better runner than passer which should fit this offense.  RB Quinton Jackson was good last year and is back.  The OL ranks #5 in production metrics, very experienced unit that jhas 5 experienced starters and should improve this fall.  Defense was last in forcing turnovers last fall with just 7, a point of emphasis this year under 2nd year DC Jon Kay. DL Chibby Nwajuaku a good anchor; have experience at DT and LBs were a pleasant surprise in spring ball. The secondary may be the best unit on defense with portal adds at safety like Koa Akui from Sacramento State.   Rice ranked very low in most metrics last season, winning five games was a credit to coaching.  This years team looks improved on offense, defense is a much bigger question.
team_notes.south-florida: Tier 1-2 AAC team; Hired Brian Hartline who was a long time WR coach at Ohio State.  Portal QBs Michael Van Buren Jr. and Luke Kromenhoek will battle for starter job - treat as unresolved.  WRs Kenny Odom and Mudia Reuben are a good duo; RB rebuilt with FCS portals but looks productive.  The OL is ranked #2 in the G6 in player production with 6 portal adds with starting experience, should be a strength.  Defense is a big question, the front seven is light on experience & production and they have concerns about pass rush; the secondary is mostly portal adds with experience but limited production.  The offense should be fun, the defense is the question of how far USF goes this fall under this new staff.
